entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop species being analyzed for its susceptibility to ergot in relation to cytoplasmic male sterility

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A genetic trait where male sterility is controlled by the cytoplasm, used in the production of hybrid seeds

entities.name: ERGOT SUSCEPTIBILITY
entities.description: The vulnerability of the plant to infection by the ergot fungus, measured by severity

entities.name: CLAVICEPS FUSIFORMIS
entities.description: The specific fungal organism responsible for causing ergot disease in pearl millet

entities.name: CMS LINES
entities.description: Cytoplasmic male-sterile lines, also known as A lines, used as female parents in hybrid breeding

entities.name: MAINTAINER LINES
entities.description: Lines, also known as B lines, that are genetically identical to CMS lines except for the cytoplasm, used to maintain CMS lines

entities.name: POLLINATOR LINES
entities.description: Lines, also known as R lines, used as male parents to pollinate CMS lines for hybrid production

entities.name: F1 HYBRIDS
entities.description: First-generation offspring resulting from crosses between A x R or B x R lines

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC FACTORS
entities.description: Genetic or physiological factors associated with the cytoplasm that influence flowering and disease susceptibility

entities.name: NUCLEAR FACTORS
entities.description: Genetic factors located in the nucleus that influence flowering and disease susceptibility

entities.name: FLOWERING EVENTS
entities.description: The biological processes of flowering that can be influenced by genetic factors and in turn affect ergot susceptibility

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The agricultural method of developing improved plant varieties, specifically CMS-based F1 hybrids

relationships.description: show lower severity than CMS lines
show susceptibility based on cytoplasmic origin
